description: Plain text document with character encoding support

technicalDetails.byteOrderMark: Optional (UTF-8: EF BB BF, UTF-16LE: FF FE)

fileStructure.body: Sequence of characters with line breaks

securityConsiderations: May contain sensitive information in plain form
Can be easily read and modified
Should be secured at filesystem level
bestPractices: Use UTF-8 encoding for international characters
Consistent line endings (LF for Unix, CRLF for Windows)
Include BOM for UTF-8 if compatibility needed
Limit line length to 80-120 characters for readability

blocks.name: BOM (Byte Order Mark)
blocks.description: UTF-8 BOM (EF BB BF) if present

blocks.name: Text Content
blocks.description: Plain text content with character encoding

nullBytes.description: Plain text should not contain null bytes

detectEncoding.description: Detect BOM or analyze byte patterns for UTF-8/UTF-16

analyzeLineEndings.description: Count LF (0x0A), CRLF (0x0D 0x0A), CR (0x0D) to determine line ending style

countLines.description: Count total number of lines in the file

parseLines.description: Split content into individual lines for display

statisticsToCollect.name: Longest Line
statisticsToCollect.description: Length of longest line in characters

exportFormats.name: UTF-8 (No BOM)
exportFormats.description: Plain text with UTF-8 encoding, no byte order mark

exportFormats.name: UTF-8 with BOM
exportFormats.description: Plain text with UTF-8 encoding and BOM (EF BB BF)

exportFormats.name: UTF-16 LE
exportFormats.description: Plain text with UTF-16 Little Endian encoding

exportFormats.name: Markdown
exportFormats.description: Convert to Markdown format with basic formatting

commonIssues.solution: Try different encodings: UTF-8, ISO-8859-1, Windows-1252

commonIssues.cause: Mixed LF and CRLF in same file
commonIssues.solution: Normalize to single line ending style

commonIssues.cause: UTF-8 BOM shown as characters in some editors
commonIssues.solution: Remove BOM or use BOM-aware editor

commonIssues.cause: Very large files may be slow to open
commonIssues.solution: Use streaming or chunk-based reading for large files

notes: This format definition handles plain text files with various encodings. The detection relies on content analysis rather than magic bytes, as plain text has no standard signature. The multi-tier detection system will analyze the text ratio and encoding validity to determine if a file is plain text.
